title: Lesson 25 — Sands of Time

words.definition: lasting only a short time; impermanent
words.example: A transient fame that faded within a single season.

words.definition: lasting for a very short time
words.example: A fleeting glimpse of the comet, then it was gone.

words.definition: quickly fading from sight, memory, or existence
words.example: The evanescent glow of fireflies over the lawn.
A transitory phase the child soon outgrew.

words.definition: lasting or recurring through many years; enduring
words.example: A perennial favorite that never goes out of style.

words.definition: lasting over a long period; durable
words.example: An enduring friendship that spanned forty years.

words.definition: occurring every day; ordinary or commonplace
words.example: The quotidian chores of an ordinary working week.

words.definition: a thing belonging or appropriate to a period other than the one in which it exists
words.example: A rotary phone is a charming anachronism today.

words.definition: very old or old-fashioned; belonging to an earlier period
words.example: Archaic spellings dot the crumbling manuscript.

words.definition: no longer produced or used; out of date
words.example: Floppy disks are now completely obsolete.
Antiquated rules that no one actually follows anymore.
The ceaseless roar of the waterfall filled the valley.

story: The night watchman loved the museum's contrasts. Some exhibits felt [[transient|transient]] — [[fleeting|fleeting]], [[evanescent|evanescent]], [[transitory|transitory]] fashions that lasted a decade and died. Others were [[perennial|perennial]] and [[enduring|enduring]], as familiar as the [[quotidian|quotidian]] hum of the city outside. A wall clock that no longer ticked was a charming [[anachronism|anachronism]], its [[archaic|archaic]] face marking hours no one kept. Around it sat [[obsolete|obsolete]] machines and [[antiquated|antiquated]] tools — and beneath them all, the [[ceaseless|ceaseless]] drip of a leaking pipe, the one clock that never stopped.
